title: CLIâ†”opsâ†”scripts parity survey â€” categorical CLI-side parity under frozen in-pi ops
question: Given pi-context's three surfaces over one library (the 48 surface:"use" ops, the CLI that reflects them 1:1, and the orchestrator-script twins), why do CLI defects keep surfacing, and can the CLI be brought to categorical capability-parity with the scripts WITHOUT changing in-pi op behavior â€” enumerating every script capability and the CLI seam that closes each gap?
method: Agent-produced comprehensive read-only survey (general-purpose, opus) over packages/pi-context/src (ops-registry.ts, read-element.ts, context.ts, block-api.ts), packages/pi-context-cli/src (bin.ts, cli.ts), scripts/orchestrator/*.ts, and scripts/parity-check.ts. Enumerated all three surfaces; built a capability matrix; categorized each parity gap under the hard constraint that in-pi op behavior is frozen. Two known gaps (FGAP-019, FGAP-020) reproduced empirically against the live .context substrate. Orchestrator independently re-verified the load-bearing counts (48 use-ops confirmed via CLI --help) and read the verdict/categorization sections directly.
scope: scripts/orchestrator/*.ts op-twin + composer scripts (orchestrator counted 53 .ts files vs the survey's 52-file partition â€” a 1-file discrepancy left unreconciled)
findings_summary:
Three surfaces sit over the same block-api/context-sdk library: the in-pi OPS (48 surface:"use" OpDefinitions), the CLI (a 1:1 runtime reflection of the ops, cli.ts:44), and the orchestrator SCRIPTS (hand-ergonomic twins). CLI defects keep surfacing because the ops were authored for the in-pi caller (which reads schemas, retries, infers, tolerates verbose output), the CLI relays them faithfully, and the scripts add ergonomics the ops never had â€” so every latent op assumption becomes CLI friction on first direct use, invisible to unit tests that always supplied the happy-path input.

The survey enumerates 8 parity gaps where the CLI sits below script capability: GAP-1 FGAP-019 (array_key declared REQUIRED across a CLASS of 7 block-mutation ops though the script derives it); GAP-2 FGAP-020 (read-schema --path pages an addressed object node down to its incidental `required` array name-list); and 6 newly surveyed â€” GAP-3 no human-render/--format mode, GAP-4 no --show-schema contract preview, GAP-5 no AJV error translation, GAP-6 no append-time dry-run, GAP-7 no input shorthands (--writer kind:id / --where field:op:value / CSV), GAP-8 coarse exit codes (0/1/2 vs the scripts' 1-5).

CATEGORICAL VERDICT: full script-capability parity is reachable with ZERO in-pi op changes. Each gap maps to a CLI-layer seam under the frozen-ops constraint: GAP-1/GAP-7 = CLI-pre-call (derive/normalize input before the unchanged op â€” e.g. extend cli.ts's required-filter exemption from {writer} to {writer, arrayKey} and inject the derived key via a helper mirroring injectWriter); GAP-2/3/4/6/8 = CLI-only-shared-helper (a helper in-pi dispatch never touches â€” for GAP-2, re-run the addressed read with structureForRead({whole:true}) which skips collection paging and returns the full subtree while still applying the 50KB cap); GAP-5 = CLI-post-call (AJV error reshape in the cli.ts catch). No gap is forced into REQUIRES-OP-CHANGE for the CLI surface.

THE ONE RESIDUAL (a user decision): closing FGAP-020 CLI-side corrects ONLY the CLI surface â€” the in-pi op keeps returning the paged name-list for addressed object-node reads (structureForRead paging at read-element.ts:266-285). Making BOTH surfaces return the full subtree requires passing whole:true at ops-registry.ts:1166, an op change conflicting with the frozen constraint. So FGAP-020's intended fix forks: "CLI returns the subtree" (closable now, CLI-side) vs "both surfaces correct" (needs the one op change).

REGRESSION FINDING: the existing parity-check.ts dual-surface guard keys the script twin by EXACT op-name (parity-check.ts:748), so 29 of 48 use-ops â€” including the entire block-mutation family where FGAP-019 lives, plus 5 name-mismatched twins â€” are structurally invisible to it; it also checks only 3 optional params, never a required-but-derivable one, never output shape. A behavioral gate would add, after the static checks: a scriptâ†”op name map, fixtures + an execution harness, a required-but-derivable assertion (FAIL if an op's required param is one the script derives), and an output-shape diff (the read-schema --path case FAILs today: opâ†’name-list, scriptâ†’subtree). This converts the class from found-one-at-a-time-by-dogfooding into caught-at-commit.

Caveat carried forward: the 53-vs-52 orchestrator-script count discrepancy is unreconciled â€” the script inventory should not be treated as exhaustive until resolved.

dependencies: structureForRead/discoverArrayKey/resolveCollection collection-paging semantics (read-element.ts) â€” load-bearing for GAP-2 and its whole:true CLI-side fix

stale_conditions: The CLI gains a pre-call input-derivation or post-call output-reshaping seam (cli.ts) â€” the gap list and CLI-side categorization are realized and the 'below script parity' framing is superseded.
Any of the 8 gaps is closed (FGAP-019/020 especially) â€” re-derive the residual set.

In-pi op behavior is unfrozen / an op is changed (e.g. whole:true at ops-registry.ts:1166, or a dryRun param added to append-block-item) â€” the frozen-ops constraint the verdict rests on no longer holds.
parity-check.ts gains a behavioral phase or a scriptâ†”op name map (parity-check.ts:738/748) â€” the regression-gate finding is addressed.
structureForRead's collection-paging heuristic (read-element.ts:266-285) changes â€” GAP-2's root locus and the whole:true fix shift.
The orchestrator-script inventory changes (or the 53-vs-52 count is reconciled) â€” the script-twin enumeration must be re-derived.
cli.ts's reflection machinery (useOps filter :44, required-from-schema :277-282, injectWriter :293-303) is refactored such that the named seams move.

citations.label: CLI required-flag check + writer exemption (GAP-1 pre-call seam)
read-schema op wraps addressed node in structureForRead (GAP-2 root locus / the op-change point)
structureForRead collection-paging that projects an object node (FGAP-020)
script returns full schema subtree directly (FGAP-020 contrast)
parity-check dual-surface guard keys twin by exact op-name (the 29/48 blind spot)
